strategy_spec.scorer: E2 (LEAD-CAGR): 50/50 of WIN1 (ml_3plus6-select / blend-trigger) + RC-D (regime-conditional-blend-select / ml_3plus6-trigger + conviction-adaptive breadth), chronos_p70_filter
strategy_spec.scorer_description: E2 (deployed 2026-05-17, 'LEAD-CAGR') is a 50/50 monthly-rebalanced portfolio of two sleeves of the SAME GBM ml+consensus alpha. Sleeve A (WIN1, unchanged): stock-SELECTION = mean(3m,6m) forward-rank (ml_3plus6); rebalance-TIMING trigger = 50/50 blend of the multi-horizon consensus rank and the ml_3plus6 rank. Sleeve B (RC D + adaptive breadth): TIMING trigger = ml_3plus6; SELECTION = the consensus/ml blend whose weight is REGIME-CONDITIONAL — momentum-leaning (consensus weight 0.30) in a confirmed SPY bull, consensus-stable (0.60) in normal/recovery — AND the basket breadth is CONVICTION-ADAPTIVE: hold the top 2 when the cross-sectional score gap signals genuine conviction, widen to the top 3 when scores are bunched (the picker is guessing). Both sleeves pick from the Chronos-p70-filtered PIT pool (Chronos-bolt-tiny zero-shot, must rank top 55%); the combined live book is up to ~6 names at 0.5x each sleeve's inverse-vol weights. The two sleeves rebalance at different times / hold different names, so their idiosyncratic variance decorrelates while the shared alpha compounds (E1's free-consistency lever) AND Sleeve B adds regime-timed return + adaptive-breadth consistency (orthogonal levers that stack). E2 is a STRICT improvement on the prior single-deploy E1 over the canonical pipeline: full CAGR 51.9%->56.6%, Sharpe 1.03->1.10, Max DD -56% (unchanged), WF 10/10 beat SPY, all 4 eras beat S&P-DCA, worst rolling-5y DCA +11.7%->+13.6%/yr, forward CAGR excluding 2003-09 33.4%->38.2%, cost-insensitive, wide mix-weight plateau, strongest out-of-sample holdout of any variant (untouched 2013-26 Sharpe ~1.24). See spx_pit/IMPROVE_PICK_RCD_FINDINGS.md and IMPROVE_PICK_RCE1_FINDINGS.md.
strategy_spec.regime_gate_rule: crash if SPY 21d <= -8% OR (SPY 6m <= -5% AND SPY 21d <= -3%); recovery if SPY below 200dma streak >= 40d AND SPY just back above 200dma AND SPY 21d > 0; bull if SPY 12m >= 10% AND above 200dma; else normal.
strategy_spec.universe: PIT S&P 500 members at each rebalance month-end (iShares IVV current holdings used for live universe)
strategy_spec.rebalance_rule: Each of the two E2 sleeves holds its basket at least 6 months, then rebalances ONLY when none of its current picks is still in that sleeve's new top-K eligible pool (its own drift scorer has discovered the held names are no longer best). Force rebalance at 24 months max. Always rebalance on regime crash transition. Within each sleeve's rebalance, weights = 1/vol_1y per pick, capped at 40% and renormalized; the two sleeves are held 50/50 and rebalanced to parity monthly, so the live book is up to ~6 names. Sleeve A is the unchanged WIN1 (select=ml_3plus6, trigger=blend). Sleeve B selects the top 2-3 (conviction-adaptive) by a regime-conditional consensus/ml blend (consensus weight 0.30 in confirmed bull, 0.60 in normal/recovery) with trigger=ml_3plus6. Because the two sleeves use different drift/selection scorers they rebalance on different dates — this desynchronisation decorrelates their idiosyncratic variance while Sleeve B's regime-timed blend adds forward return and adaptive breadth adds year-to-year consistency. E2 is a strict improvement on the prior E1 (CAGR 51.9->56.6%, Sharpe 1.03->1.10, Max DD -56% unchanged, WF 10/10, worst rolling-5y DCA +11.7->+13.6%/yr, forward CAGR ex-2003-09 33.4->38.2%, 4/4 eras beat S&P-DCA). HONESTY NOTE: the canonical equity curve (and the dca_investor block) is the honest reference — Sharpe ~1.1 and still-deep interim drawdowns. The -56% drawdown is the 2008 GFC systemic event; a stock-picking lever cannot move it — E2 raises return and consistency AT E1's drawdown, it does NOT make the strategy low-risk. A monthly-DCA accumulator still sees a deep interim value drawdown and the edge magnitude is still front-loaded in 2003-2009.

chronos_filter.rule: At each rebalance, the candidate pool is restricted to stocks where the Chronos p70 3m-forecast cross-sectional rank is >= 0.45 (i.e., the upper 55%). Then v3 picks the top 2 by ml_3plus6 score from that filtered pool. This is universe-agnostic alpha that complements the GBM's tabular signal — verified on broader 1833-ticker, non-S&P 500 PIT, and random subset universes.

recommended_strategy.name: v5_pit_sp500_E2_win1_rcdadaptk_5050_chronos_p70_k2_invvol_cap0.4_minhold6_scoredrift
recommended_strategy.description: v5 PIT S&P 500 strategy. Walk-forward Gradient Boosted Trees ranking model (3m+6m forward-rank ensemble) restricted to point-in-time S&P 500 constituents at each rebalance, gated by the HuggingFace Chronos-bolt-tiny zero-shot foundation model (p70 forecast rank ≥ 0.45). Top-2 picks, inverse-volatility weighted with 40% cap per pick, held a minimum of 6 months then rebalanced only on score-drift (force at 24m); 'tight' regime gate goes 100% cash on SPY 21d <= -8% or 6m <= -5%. Lump-sum walk-forward ~49% MEAN OOS CAGR over 10 splits 2003-2025 (10/10 positive, 10/10 beat SPY) — strong engine evidence, NOT the monthly-DCA experience. The honest investor-facing result is in dca_investor: a 10-year monthly DCA beat the identical S&P-500 DCA in 100% of rolling windows, with deep (~-79%) interim drawdowns disclosed.

wf_explanation.explanation: 10 walk-forward TRAIN/TEST splits over 2003-2024 on the PIT S&P 500 universe.  The GBM is fit on TRAIN data only (annual retrain, 7-month embargo), then applied to TEST.  Reported metrics are TEST-window CAGR, edge over SPY, Sharpe, and max drawdown — all out-of-sample.

dca_investor.convention: contribute $1 at the start of each month, earn that month's return; v5 net of 10bps; PIT data, no tuning
